world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 9
major_event: Red lightning splits the sky above Tidefall at midnight — no storm, no clouds, only silent crimson forks across a clear sky. Every faction that can see the coast is now asking what it means. The Admiral has not slept.

primary_event.name: The Red Lightning Over Tidefall
primary_event.summary: At midnight, crimson lightning without thunder or cloud split the sky above Tidefall harbor. Sailors prostrated themselves. The Admiral watched from the flagship deck in silence. By dawn, every port within sight of the coast had a story, and none agreed.

supporting_events.name: Deep Entity: The Name Crescendos
supporting_events.summary: The hammering beneath Lostfeld reached a sustained pitch before dawn. Kessa Stonebraid collapsed in her quarters. Ironmaul found her speaking words in the Entity's language — words she has never been taught.

supporting_events.name: Tidefall's Offshore Coalition Takes Shape
supporting_events.summary: Two senior Twin Cities lords met the Admiral aboard the neutral vessel before dawn. A preliminary accord was signed — not a treaty, but a letter of intent aligning their succession preference with Tidefall's harbor interests.

supporting_events.name: Rowen Prepares to Dive
supporting_events.summary: Rowen has decided. He will descend to the iron door himself at the next low tide. His navigator calculates they have three days before the door drops below safe diving depth. He has told no one what he expects to find.

active_events.name: The Red Lightning Over Tidefall
active_events.summary: Crimson lightning without storm or cloud struck above Tidefall harbor at midnight. No damage, no thunder — only light and terror. Sailors refuse to sail east. The Admiral convened his officers at dawn.
active_events.consequences: Interpretations will diverge dangerously. Tidefall's enemies will claim it as condemnation. Tidefall's allies will claim it as coronation. The Druids will want to read it. The Seer has not yet seen it.

active_events.name: Deep Entity: The Name Crescendos
active_events.summary: Kessa collapsed speaking the Entity's language unprompted. Ironmaul has sealed her quarters and posted guards. The hammering is now felt as vibration in the air, not just the stone. Workers in the upper shafts are refusing shifts.
active_events.consequences: Kessa is becoming a conduit. Each tick without intervention risks losing her as a conscious agent. The Druids' partial comprehension of the name is now dangerously close to threshold.

active_events.name: Twin Cities Succession Paralysis
active_events.summary: Two senior lords have now signed a letter of intent with Tidefall. Caeris's sealed regency proclamation remains hidden. The chamber door has not opened. The red lightning over Tidefall has stunned the remaining court into superstitious paralysis.
active_events.consequences: The letter of intent is a legal instrument in embryo. If Roderic dies without acting, Caeris's unsigned proclamation faces a signed rival document. The Seer arrives tomorrow.

active_events.name: Grand Druid Reads the Name
active_events.summary: The Grand Druid's inner Conclave has now cross-referenced Ironmaul's musical notation against the pre-Accord text. The structural match is undeniable. The Grand Druid has called a halt to all reading — but two junior scholars continued through the night.
active_events.consequences: The junior scholars may have already spoken the threshold syllables aloud. If so, the gate at Lostfeld may respond before anyone is prepared. The Grand Druid does not yet know his order may have already acted.

active_events.name: Tidefall's Offshore Coalition
active_events.summary: The preliminary accord with two senior lords formalizes Tidefall's coalition strategy. Dur Khadur mercenary consultancy language is being folded into a secondary document. The red lightning complicates the Admiral's morning.
active_events.consequences: The letter of intent creates legal ambiguity if Roderic dies today. The red lightning may be read by the lords as omen against or for Tidefall — their interpretation will determine whether they hold or defect.

active_events.name: Velorn Detained, Shadow Court Fractures
active_events.summary: The Velorn clan head appeared before Lythara's private session and denied authorizing the envoy — a lie she can see but cannot prove. The Nythariel clan head said nothing. The silence was its own verdict.
active_events.consequences: Lythara cannot punish without evidence, cannot ignore without losing authority. Velorn has tested the ceiling of her power and found it lower than expected. The fracture is not yet visible — but it is structural.

active_events.name: Rowen's Key and the Submerged Door
active_events.summary: Rowen has committed to a personal dive at the next low tide. His navigator has mapped the submerged arches precisely. Whatever is behind the iron door, Rowen will reach it within two days or lose the window forever.
active_events.consequences: If the door yields, Rowen gains something whose nature is unknown. If the island descends faster than calculated, the window closes permanently. Tidefall's eastern lanes remain partially disrupted by the anchorage.

active_events.name: Gnome Syndicate: The Seer's Road
active_events.summary: The shadow-agent tailing the Seer has now observed him for a full day. He carries nothing written. He speaks to no one on the road. The agent's report: the Seer walks like a man who already knows what he will find.
active_events.consequences: The Syndicate now knows the Seer's destination and pace. They will know his message's content only if they intercept him or his first conversation with Caeris. The red lightning has complicated their calculus — new leverage may be worth more.

faction_actions.action: The Admiral convenes all officers at dawn and presents the red lightning as a sign of mandate — divine sanction for the transition of coastal power. He orders the harbor-treaty document formally drafted and dated.
faction_actions.reason: The omen must be claimed before enemies claim it against him. Speed is now essential.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities minor lords / internal fleet morale

faction_actions.action: Grand Druid sends an emergency sealed rider to Lostfeld: the Conclave has matched the hammering notation to text in the pre-Accord book. He does not yet name what it means — only that they must not respond to the Entity until he arrives.
faction_actions.reason: The junior scholars' nocturnal reading may have crossed a threshold. He must reach Lostfeld before Ironmaul acts on incomplete knowledge.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul seals the gate chamber entirely — walls it with fresh stone, posts the last loyal garrison soldiers, and moves Kessa to the upper halls under constant watch from two of his most trusted advisors.
faction_actions.reason: Kessa spoke the language. The gate must not reach her again until he understands what is happening.

faction_actions.action: Elowen releases the Velorn envoy with a formal message to Lythara: the envoy was received, treated with courtesy, and told nothing — a pointed demonstration that Elowen is above the Shadow Court's internal politics.
faction_actions.reason: Holding him longer creates obligation. Releasing him with a message reframes the situation as Elowen's graciousness, not Velorn's failure.

faction_actions.action: Lythara dispatches a covert message to the Arkenor lieutenant via a Nythariel intermediary — bypassing Velorn entirely — offering him reduced sentence in exchange for a full account of the Frostvale contact.
faction_actions.reason: She cannot trust Velorn's account. The lieutenant is the only direct source. She will not wait for Elowen's permission.

faction_actions.action: Caeris Thorne, hearing of the red lightning by morning courier from a coastal lord, breaks his vigil and walks to the court council chamber — summoning every lord still present to an emergency session in the king's name.
faction_actions.reason: The omen has shattered the court's paralysis. Caeris seizes the moment of collective shock to assert himself before interpretations harden against him.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities court / remaining minor lords

faction_actions.action: Rowen orders all diving gear inspected and his best swimmer-soldiers prepared. He announces at ship's muster that he will descend personally to the iron door at low tide tomorrow morning with four chosen men.
faction_actions.reason: Three days remain before the door is unreachable. He cannot wait for better conditions.

faction_actions.action: The Syndicate dispatches a second agent — this one ahead of the Seer on the road — to position near the Twin Cities gate and observe Caeris's reaction upon receiving the Seer's message.
faction_actions.reason: The red lightning has made Caeris's response to the Seer's message vastly more valuable. Whoever holds that reaction first holds leverage over the succession narrative.

faction_actions.action: Dur Khadur envoys immediately reframe their mercenary offer to the Admiral as 'stability enforcement' — security for the transition, not intervention — folding the red lightning into the language as a legitimizing event.
faction_actions.reason: The omen creates a rhetorical opening. A divine sign plus a security force makes Tidefall's succession play look inevitable, not aggressive.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall Admiral / Twin Cities succession framing

faction_actions.action: Farrock's garrison commander dispatches a rider south toward the Twin Cities with a report on the Lostfeld vibration anomaly — framed as a border security concern, not a supernatural event.
faction_actions.reason: The red lightning was visible from the Farrock battlements. The commander decides this is the moment to formally alert the Twin Cities and establish Farrock as a reliable partner.

faction_actions.action: The Vilefin scouting band reaches the Lostfeld foothills and halts — the warmth is now clearly felt in the air, not just the soil. The band leader sends a runner back to the Rock Plains with a description and requests more scouts before proceeding.
faction_actions.reason: The warmth is stronger than expected. The goblin band leader is cautious enough to wait for numbers before approaching the source.

faction_actions.action: Two Vampires embedded in the Twin Cities court separately approach the two lords who signed Tidefall's letter of intent — each offering a private reading of the red lightning as a warning against hasty coastal alliances.
faction_actions.reason: The lords are now frightened. Fear makes them malleable. The Vampires want the letter of intent walked back, keeping the succession fluid and their own influence central.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities minor lords / letter of intent signatories

faction_actions.action: The Seer stops at a wayside shepherd's shelter for the night. He saw the red lightning on the southern horizon and stood in the dark for a long time before going inside. He does not change his course. He will arrive tomorrow.
faction_actions.reason: He cannot interpret the lightning from here. He can only continue walking toward the place where his message was always needed most.

faction_actions.action: Rowen's navigator, watching the red lightning from the anchorage, records it in the ship's log as 'a sign above the Admiral's harbor — seen by all, understood by none.' He marks the island's waterline again at dawn: another hand-width gone.
faction_actions.reason: The navigator is methodical. He does not interpret omens, only records them. The measurement is what matters.

recent_events.text: Crimson lightning without thunder or cloud split the sky above Tidefall harbor at midnight. No damage. Sailors knelt on the docks. The Admiral watched from his flagship deck and said nothing until dawn, when he called it a mandate.

Kessa Stonebraid collapsed before dawn, speaking phrases in an unknown tongue. Ironmaul has sealed the gate chamber in fresh stone and moved her to the upper halls under guard. He is waiting for the Druids' rider.

Caeris Thorne broke his vigil when the red lightning's news arrived by courier. He has called an emergency court session in the king's name — the first royal summons issued in days. The chamber door behind him remains sealed.

Lythara received the Velorn envoy back with Elowen's courtesy message. She read it once, set it face-down on her table, and has not spoken since. The Velorn clan head left the private session without punishment and without absolution.

Rowen announced to his assembled captains that he will dive personally to the iron door at tomorrow's low tide. The island lost another hand-width of freeboard overnight. The red lightning was visible from the anchorage and has been logged.

active_tensions.factions: All Coastal Factions — The Red Lightning's Meaning
active_tensions.description: Tidefall claims mandate. Enemies see condemnation. The Druids want to read it. The Seer has not seen it. Every interpretation that hardens before the truth emerges becomes a future grievance or a future war.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld Dwarves — Deep Entity vs Kessa's Mind
active_tensions.description: Kessa is speaking the Entity's language without instruction. The gate chamber is sealed but stone is not silence. The Druids may have already spoken the threshold syllables. Time is now measured in hours, not days.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Caeris's Emergency Session vs Tidefall's Letter of Intent
active_tensions.description: Caeris seized the omen's moment to call court. The letter of intent already exists. The Seer arrives tomorrow. Whichever act lands first — the regency proclamation or the coalition document — defines what legitimacy means in this succession.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Lythara vs Velorn's Quiet Ceiling-Test
active_tensions.description: Velorn denied the envoy without evidence of a lie. Lythara cannot punish. The Nythariel silence was not loyalty — it was assessment. The fracture is structural and invisible, which is the most dangerous kind.

character_updates.name: Seer
character_updates.status: Sheltering overnight at a wayside shepherd's hut on the mountain road. Saw the red lightning on the southern horizon. Did not change course. Arrives at Twin Cities tomorrow. Gnome shadow-agent is one ridge behind him.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Collapsed before dawn, spoke the Entity's language aloud without apparent awareness. Now confined to upper halls under guard. Her palms show symbols. She has not regained full lucidity.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Sealed the gate chamber with fresh stone. Awaiting the Druid's emergency rider. Has not eaten. Holds the shape of command while the crisis narrows around him.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Broke his vigil. Called an emergency court session. The regency proclamation is still under the floorboard. He does not yet know the Seer arrives tomorrow or that Tidefall has a signed letter of intent.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Received Elowen's courtesy release of the Velorn envoy. Has said nothing since reading it. Dispatched a covert message to the Arkenor lieutenant via Nythariel. She is recalculating, not retreating.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Announced personal dive to the iron door at tomorrow's low tide. Saw the red lightning from the anchorage. Has said nothing about what it means. He holds the key in his left hand while he briefs his dive team.

faction_morale.reason: The Admiral has claimed the red lightning as mandate. The letter of intent is signed. His inner circle believes the succession is already decided in their favor.

Kessa collapsed speaking the Entity's language. The gate is sealed but the Entity is not. Workers in upper shafts are refusing shifts. Ironmaul is running out of authority that has not already been tested by fear.
Two senior lords have signed with Tidefall. Caeris's emergency session is a gamble on omen-fear. The court does not know whether to follow him or wait to see who the lightning chose.
Lythara cannot punish Velorn. Elowen's courtesy message was a public diminishment. The Nythariel silence is being read by clan observers as a signal that the Queen's grip may be loosening.

The Grand Druid now knows the linguistic match is real. The emergency rider to Lostfeld is a declaration of engagement. The Conclave feels the weight of being the only ones who may understand what is coming.
Rowen's personal commitment to the dive has unified his captains completely. The red lightning was visible from the anchorage and is being interpreted as a sign that this is the right moment.
Two agents are now positioned around the Seer's trajectory. The red lightning has made Caeris's first reaction to the Seer an enormously valuable intelligence commodity.
The Grand Druid has matched the hammering notation to the pre-Accord text. The emergency rider dispatched. The Conclave feels the terrible clarity of finally understanding the shape of the danger.

faction_resources.pressure: Lythara's covert Nythariel channel costs gold and trust. Velorn insult unresolved. Influence draining from clan fracture perception.
Envoy release with courtesy message costs nothing and gains influence over Shadow Court optics. Druid emissary still en route — ongoing gold drain.
Two lords signed with Tidefall. Emergency court session costs political capital. Red lightning has stunned remaining court — influence continues to bleed.
Letter of intent signed. Red lightning claimed as mandate. Gold spent on offshore meeting recovered in influence gains. Surge in morale and fleet readiness.
Gate sealed — materials cost. More workers refusing shifts — food and military declining. Kessa's collapse is the deepest morale wound yet. Druid rider incoming but not yet arrived.
Second agent deployed — gold and military cost. Red lightning has elevated value of Caeris intelligence. Net influence gain expected next tick.
Emergency rider to Lostfeld costs gold and materials. Grand Druid may be preparing to travel personally — further drain anticipated. Rising influence from perceived authority on Entity crisis.
Dive preparation costs materials. Extended anchorage ongoing food drain. Red lightning interpreted positively — morale offsets supply pressure.
Red lightning reframe costs narrative effort but no hard resources. Mercenary consultancy language now folded into letter of intent documents — influence rising with Tidefall's ascent.
Rider dispatched to Twin Cities costs gold. Border watch maintained. Red lightning visible from battlements — garrison unease manageable but noted.
Scout band halted at foothills — food drain ongoing, no return yet. Runner sent back costs military. Heat anomaly increasing caution. Red lightning not visible from Rock Plains position.
Two Vampires deployed to letter-of-intent lords costs gold. Red lightning is the best harvesting environment they have seen — fear and uncertainty multiply influence returns.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
